purpose: Choose the next C106-ready route after C104 showed weak SigLIP2 hard-shape separation.

c102_local_real_positive_gate.interpretation: Local-real direct green training is not safe without manual or external annotation because no confirmed positives were found.

qwen_baseline_evidence.interpretation: QwenVL-side C087 remains the strongest hard-shape reference-control baseline among completed C098 variants.

c098_siglip_encoder_lora_evidence.interpretation: The deeper SigLIP encoder-LoRA trained and generated, but it remained below the Qwen hard-shape baseline and did not solve non-human heldout07.

c104_expanded_siglip_probe.interpretation: Current SigLIP2 layer -6 token space does not separate positives from explicit negatives strongly enough to justify another SigLIP-only training run.

rationale: heldout07 non-human green side-profile has no positive uplift for any tested encoder.

pros: QwenVL-side C087 is the strongest current hard-shape baseline.
C102 proves local positives are unavailable, so teacher-derived supervision is safer than local direct-green training.
C104 shows SigLIP-only token separation is weak, so C106 should use Qwen/VL teacher targets or features instead of repeating SigLIP-only LoRA.
risks: C064 shows QwenVL is only partial on hard heldout failures.
C106 must keep heldout rows evaluation-only and use explicit negatives to avoid overfitting green humanoid collapse.

pros: Native ComfyUI SigLIP patch path already exists.
C097 explicit negatives are clean and usable.
risks: C098 and C104 both show insufficient SigLIP-side signal.
A larger SigLIP-only run is likely redundant without a better teacher or checkpoint.

pros: Could create conflict-free real positives for C102-style local training.
Would directly address the current zero-local-positive blocker.
risks: Requires manual work or external rights review before autonomous training.
Not immediately runnable as the next loop without new labeled data.
